bank: If Headline 1 contains more positive or negative emotional words than Headline 2, predict Headline 1 has more clicks.
If Headline 2 contains more positive or negative emotional words than Headline 1, predict Headline 2 has more clicks.
If Headline 1 includes more concrete details (numbers, names, dates) than Headline 2, predict Headline 1 has more clicks.
If Headline 2 includes more concrete details (numbers, names, dates) than Headline 1, predict Headline 2 has more clicks.
If Headline 1 contains a question or question-like phrasing and Headline 2 does not, predict Headline 1 has more clicks.
If Headline 2 contains a question or question-like phrasing and Headline 1 does not, predict Headline 2 has more clicks.
If Headline 1 uses cliffhanger punctuation (e.g., ellipses) and Headline 2 does not, predict Headline 1 has more clicks.
If Headline 2 uses cliffhanger punctuation (e.g., ellipses) and Headline 1 does not, predict Headline 2 has more clicks.
If Headline 1 has a moderate length (e.g., 8-15 words) compared to Headline 2 being very short or very long, predict Headline 1 has more clicks.
If Headline 2 has a moderate length (e.g., 8-15 words) compared to Headline 1 being very short or very long, predict Headline 2 has more clicks.
If Headline 1 has a lower readability score (simpler language) than Headline 2, predict Headline 1 has more clicks.
If Headline 2 has a lower readability score (simpler language) than Headline 1, predict Headline 2 has more clicks.
If Headline 1 contains vague or overly complex wording compared to Headline 2, predict Headline 2 has more clicks.
If Headline 2 contains vague or overly complex wording compared to Headline 1, predict Headline 1 has more clicks.
If Headline 1 simultaneously combines emotional words, specific details, and curiosity triggers more than Headline 2, predict Headline 1 has more clicks.
